# Screenshot checklist

Two kinds of captures document this project. Terminal captures are generated
automatically as SVG (they render on GitHub and never go blurry). Browser
captures need a human with a browser; instructions below.

## Auto-generated terminal captures (SVG)

```
uv run python scripts/make_screenshots.py
```

| # | File | Shows | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `docs/assets/inspect.svg` | Model inspection table | generated by script |
| 2 | `docs/assets/tokenize.svg` | Tokenizer breakdown table | generated by script |
| 3 | `docs/assets/generate.svg` | Generation with stats footer | generated by script |
| 4 | `docs/assets/simulate.svg` | Continuous batching timeline | generated by script |
| 5 | `docs/assets/bench-report.svg` | Benchmark report with KV speedup | generated by script |

Rerun the script after changing CLI output. It uses distilgpt2 by default;
pass `--fast` to use tiny-gpt2.

## Browser captures (PNG)

The PNGs currently in `docs/assets/` were captured headlessly (Playwright
driving Chromium against a live server). To recapture by hand, start the
server first:

```
uv run mini-vllm serve --model distilbert/distilgpt2
```

Seed the data tabs before shooting:

```
uv run mini-vllm bench --model distilbert/distilgpt2 --requests 8 --compare-kv-cache
uv run mini-vllm simulate examples/traffic.json --model distilbert/distilgpt2
```

| # | File | How to capture |
|---|---|---|
| 6 | `docs/assets/playground.png` | `/dashboard`, Playground tab. Type a prompt, enable streaming, click Generate, shoot mid-stream or right after the metrics chips fill in. |
| 7 | `docs/assets/tokenizer.png` | Tokenizer tab. Tokenize "The greenhouse robot inspected the tomatoes." so the colored chips and table are populated. |
| 8 | `docs/assets/benchmarks.png` | Benchmarks tab after a bench run. The KV cache bars and continuous batching bars are the money shot. |
| 9 | `docs/assets/scheduler.png` | Scheduler tab after a simulate run. Occupancy chart plus the request gantt. |
| 10 | `docs/assets/api-docs.png` | `http://127.0.0.1:8000/docs`, expand POST /v1/completions. |
| 11 | `docs/assets/tests.png` | Terminal running `uv run pytest -v` scrolled to the green summary (or capture as SVG with the script pattern). |

Capture at a window width around 1200 px, dark OS theme, default zoom.
macOS: Cmd-Shift-4, then space, then click the window for a clean shot with
no desktop clutter.

## Where they are used

`README.md` embeds 1 to 9. The blog post draft and the demo video follow
`docs/demo_script.md`, which replays the same commands in the same order.
